summary: Provision AWS Core Infrastructure for ECS Deployment
description: Provision all foundational AWS resources required to run LoanFlow 360 on ECS Fargate. This epic covers VPC networking, ECR image repositories, managed backing services (RDS MySQL, ElastiCache Redis, MSK Kafka, S3, DocumentDB), and IAM roles for Jenkins and ECS task execution. All resources are provisioned with environment isolation in mind: dev and production resources are segregated by naming convention and VPC configuration. No application code is deployed in this epic — the output is a fully wired, reachable infrastructure baseline that the CI/CD pipeline can target.
functionality: provision the complete AWS resource set required to host all LoanFlow 360 microservices on ECS Fargate with managed backing services and least-privilege IAM access
benefit: establish a reproducible, environment-isolated infrastructure baseline that the Jenkins pipeline can deploy into without manual AWS console intervention

stories.summary: Provision VPC, subnets, gateways, and security groups
stories.description:
Create the network foundation for all LoanFlow 360 ECS workloads. This includes a dedicated VPC with public and private subnets across two availability zones, an internet gateway for the public tier, a NAT gateway for outbound access from private subnets, route tables for each subnet tier, and security groups scoped to the ALB, ECS tasks, RDS, ElastiCache, MSK, and DocumentDB. All ECS tasks run in private subnets; only the ALB is placed in public subnets.

Resource naming convention: loanflow-{env}-{resource} (e.g. loanflow-dev-vpc, loanflow-prod-ecs-sg)

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ECS tasks in private subnets reach the internet via NAT
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ECS task is running in a private subnet
acceptanceCriteria.when: the task makes an outbound HTTPS request (e.g. to pull from ECR or call Secrets Manager)
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request routes through the NAT gateway and succeeds without assigning a public IP to the task

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Security groups allow only required inbound traffic per resource tier
acceptanceCriteria.given: the ECS task security group is applied to a running service
acceptanceCriteria.when: a connection attempt arrives from outside the VPC directly on a service port
acceptanceCriteria.then: the connection is rejected; only traffic originating from the ALB security group is permitted on the service port

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: RDS and ElastiCache are reachable only from ECS task security group
acceptanceCriteria.given: the RDS security group allows inbound on port 3306 only from the ECS task security group
acceptanceCriteria.when: an ECS task connects to RDS
acceptanceCriteria.then: the connection succeeds; a connection attempt from outside the ECS task security group is denied

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Public and private subnets exist across two AZs
acceptanceCriteria.then: two public subnets and two private subnets exist, each in a different availability zone within the same region

tasks.summary: Create VPC with DNS hostname and resolution enabled
tasks.description: Provision a VPC with a /16 CIDR block (e.g. 10.0.0.0/16) with enableDnsHostnames and enableDnsSupport set to true. These settings are required for ECS tasks to resolve ECR and Secrets Manager endpoints and for RDS hostname resolution.

tasks.summary: Create public and private subnets across two availability zones
tasks.description: Create two public subnets (/24 CIDRs) and two private subnets (/24 CIDRs), each in a distinct AZ. Tag public subnets with kubernetes.io/role/elb and private subnets with kubernetes.io/role/internal-elb for forward compatibility. Associate public subnets with the internet-facing route table.

tasks.summary: Provision internet gateway, NAT gateway, and route tables
tasks.description: Attach an internet gateway to the VPC. Provision a NAT gateway in one public subnet with an associated Elastic IP. Create a public route table with a 0.0.0.0/0 route to the internet gateway and a private route table with a 0.0.0.0/0 route to the NAT gateway. Associate subnets with their respective route tables.

tasks.summary: Define security groups for ALB, ECS tasks, RDS, ElastiCache, MSK, and DocumentDB
tasks.description:
Create the following security groups:
- loanflow-{env}-alb-sg: inbound 80/443 from 0.0.0.0/0
- loanflow-{env}-ecs-sg: inbound on service ports from alb-sg only
- loanflow-{env}-rds-sg: inbound 3306 from ecs-sg only
- loanflow-{env}-redis-sg: inbound 6379 from ecs-sg only
- loanflow-{env}-msk-sg: inbound 9092 from ecs-sg only
- loanflow-{env}-docdb-sg: inbound 27017 from ecs-sg only
All security groups allow all outbound traffic.

stories.summary: Provision ECR repositories for all six microservices
stories.description:
Create one Amazon ECR repository per LoanFlow 360 microservice with image tag immutability and vulnerability scanning enabled. Repositories are used by the Jenkins pipeline to push and pull versioned service images during CI/CD runs. A lifecycle policy is applied to each repository to expire untagged images after 7 days and retain a maximum of 20 tagged images, preventing unbounded storage growth.

Repositories to create:
- loanflow/api-gateway
- loanflow/identity-service
- loanflow/loan-service
- loanflow/document-service
- loanflow/underwriting-service
- loanflow/notification-service

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: One ECR repository exists for each of the six microservices
acceptanceCriteria.when: the ECR repository list is inspected in the AWS console or via CLI
acceptanceCriteria.then: exactly six repositories exist under the loanflow/ prefix, one per service

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Image tag immutability prevents overwriting an existing tag
acceptanceCriteria.given: an image with tag abc1234 has been pushed to a repository
acceptanceCriteria.when: a second push attempts to overwrite the same tag abc1234
acceptanceCriteria.then: ECR rejects the push with an ImageTagAlreadyExistsException

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Lifecycle policy expires untagged images after 7 days
acceptanceCriteria.given: an untagged image has existed in a repository for 8 days
acceptanceCriteria.when: the ECR lifecycle policy evaluates the repository
acceptanceCriteria.then: the untagged image is deleted and no longer appears in the repository image list

tasks.summary: Create six ECR repositories with tag immutability and scan-on-push enabled
tasks.description: Provision repositories for api-gateway, identity-service, loan-service, document-service, underwriting-service, and notification-service under the loanflow/ namespace. Set imageTagMutability to IMMUTABLE and imageScanningConfiguration scanOnPush to true on each repository.

tasks.summary: Apply ECR lifecycle policy to each repository
tasks.description: Attach a lifecycle policy to each repository with two rules: (1) expire untagged images with countType sinceImagePushed greater than 7 days, and (2) retain only the most recent 20 tagged images. Apply policies via the AWS CLI or infrastructure-as-code to ensure repeatability.

tasks.summary: Output ECR repository URIs for use in Jenkins environment variables
tasks.description: After provisioning, document the full ECR registry URI (account-id.dkr.ecr.region.amazonaws.com) and repository names so they can be configured as Jenkins global environment variables (ECR_REGISTRY, AWS_REGION) referenced by the Jenkinsfile.

stories.summary: Provision RDS MySQL instances and ElastiCache Redis cluster
stories.description:
Provision the relational and caching backing services required by the transactional LoanFlow 360 microservices. Four of the six services (identity-service, loan-service, document-service, underwriting-service) use MySQL for persistence. The api-gateway and loan-service use Redis for distributed rate limiting and application summary caching respectively.

RDS instances are placed in private subnets with Multi-AZ disabled for dev and enabled for production. ElastiCache Redis is deployed as a single-node cluster for dev and a replication group for production. All credentials are stored in AWS Secrets Manager at provisioning time.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Each transactional service has a dedicated RDS database
acceptanceCriteria.when: the RDS instance list is inspected
acceptanceCriteria.then: four RDS MySQL instances exist, one each for identity-service, loan-service, document-service, and underwriting-service, each in the private subnet group

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: RDS instances are not publicly accessible
acceptanceCriteria.when: a connection attempt is made from outside the VPC on port 3306
acceptanceCriteria.then: the connection is refused; the instance endpoint is not resolvable from the public internet

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ElastiCache Redis cluster endpoint is reachable from ECS tasks
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ECS task running in the private subnet attempts to connect to Redis on port 6379
acceptanceCriteria.when: the connection is made using the ElastiCache primary endpoint
acceptanceCriteria.then: the connection succeeds and a PING returns PONG

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: RDS and Redis credentials are stored in Secrets Manager at creation time
acceptanceCriteria.given: the RDS and ElastiCache provisioning scripts complete
acceptanceCriteria.then: secrets exist at /loanflow/{env}/{service-name}/db-password and /loanflow/{env}/redis-auth-token with the generated credentials

tasks.summary: Create RDS subnet group covering private subnets in both AZs
tasks.description: Create a DB subnet group named loanflow-{env}-db-subnet-group using the two private subnets. This subnet group is referenced by all four RDS instances to ensure instances are placed in the private network tier.

tasks.summary: Provision four RDS MySQL 8.0 instances, one per transactional service
tasks.description: Create RDS instances for identity-service, loan-service, document-service, and underwriting-service. Use db.t3.micro for dev and db.t3.small for production. Set MultiAZ to false for dev and true for production. Attach the rds-sg security group. Enable automated backups with a 7-day retention window. Set the master username and generate a password, storing it in Secrets Manager at /loanflow/{env}/{service-name}/db-password.

tasks.summary: Provision ElastiCache Redis 7.x cluster in private subnets
tasks.description: Create an ElastiCache subnet group using the private subnets. Provision a Redis cluster (cache.t3.micro for dev, cache.t3.small for production) with in-transit encryption enabled and an auth token stored in Secrets Manager at /loanflow/{env}/redis-auth-token. Attach the redis-sg security group.

tasks.summary: Document RDS endpoints and Redis primary endpoint for Spring Boot config
tasks.description: After provisioning, record each RDS instance endpoint hostname and the ElastiCache primary endpoint. These values are stored in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store at /loanflow/{env}/{service-name}/db-host and /loanflow/{env}/redis-host so they can be injected as environment variables in ECS task definitions without hardcoding in application config files.

stories.summary: Provision MSK Kafka cluster, S3 document bucket, and DocumentDB cluster
stories.description:
Provision the messaging, object storage, and document-oriented backing services used by the event-driven microservices in LoanFlow 360. MSK provides the Kafka broker for the loan.application.submitted, loan.document.verified, loan.underwriting.completed, and loan.status.updated topics. S3 stores document files uploaded by the document-service. DocumentDB hosts the append-heavy audit events and notifications consumed by the notification-service.

All three resources are placed in private subnets. MSK uses SASL/SCRAM authentication; S3 uses server-side encryption; DocumentDB uses TLS with credentials in Secrets Manager.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: MSK cluster brokers are reachable from ECS tasks on port 9092
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ECS task running in the private subnet attempts to connect to the MSK bootstrap broker
acceptanceCriteria.when: a Kafka producer sends a test message to the loan.application.submitted topic
acceptanceCriteria.then: the message is acknowledged without connection errors

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: S3 bucket blocks all public access
acceptanceCriteria.when: a public access check is performed via the AWS console or CLI
acceptanceCriteria.then: BlockPublicAcls, IgnorePublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, and RestrictPublicBuckets are all set to true

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: DocumentDB cluster is reachable from ECS tasks on port 27017
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ECS task running in the private subnet connects to the DocumentDB cluster endpoint
acceptanceCriteria.when: a MongoDB-compatible PING command is sent
acceptanceCriteria.then: the cluster responds successfully over TLS

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: MSK SASL credentials and DocumentDB password are stored in Secrets Manager
acceptanceCriteria.given: MSK and DocumentDB provisioning is complete

tasks.summary: Provision MSK Kafka cluster with two brokers across two AZs
tasks.description: Create an MSK cluster named loanflow-{env}-kafka using Kafka 3.x with two broker nodes (kafka.t3.small for dev, kafka.m5.large for production), one per AZ in the private subnets. Enable SASL/SCRAM authentication and in-transit encryption (TLS). Store SASL credentials in Secrets Manager at /loanflow/{env}/msk-sasl-credentials. Attach the msk-sg security group.

tasks.summary: Create Kafka topics for the four LoanFlow 360 event streams
tasks.description: After the MSK cluster reaches ACTIVE state, create the following topics using the Kafka CLI or MSK API: loan.application.submitted (3 partitions, replication factor 2), loan.document.verified (3 partitions, RF 2), loan.underwriting.completed (3 partitions, RF 2), loan.status.updated (3 partitions, RF 2).

tasks.summary: Provision S3 bucket for document-service with versioning and SSE-S3 encryption
tasks.description: Create an S3 bucket named loanflow-{env}-documents with versioning enabled, server-side encryption set to SSE-S3, and all public access blocked. Add a lifecycle rule to transition objects older than 90 days to S3 Infrequent Access storage class. Record the bucket name in Parameter Store at /loanflow/{env}/s3-documents-bucket.

Create a DocumentDB subnet group using the private subnets. Provision a DocumentDB cluster (db.t3.medium) with TLS enabled, automated backups with a 7-day retention, and the master password stored in Secrets Manager at /loanflow/{env}/docdb-password. Attach the docdb-sg security group. Record the cluster endpoint in Parameter Store at /loanflow/{env}/docdb-host.

stories.summary: Configure IAM roles for Jenkins EC2 and ECS task execution
stories.description: Define and attach the IAM roles required for the Jenkins EC2 instance to perform ECR pushes and ECS deployments, and for ECS task execution to pull images from ECR and inject secrets from Secrets Manager at container startup. Both roles follow least-privilege principles: the Jenkins role grants only the actions needed for the CI/CD pipeline; the ECS task execution role grants only the actions needed to start and run a container. Application-level AWS access (e.g. S3 reads from the document-service) is handled by a separate ECS task role, which is out of scope for this story.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Jenkins pushes images and triggers ECS deployments without stored AWS keys
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Jenkins EC2 instance has the Jenkins IAM role attached via instance profile
acceptanceCriteria.when: the pipeline runs ECR login, docker push, and aws ecs update-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: all three operations succeed using the instance metadata service and no AWS access key IDs are stored in Jenkins credential store

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Jenkins IAM role is denied access to resources outside its policy
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Jenkins IAM role policy is evaluated
acceptanceCriteria.when: the role attempts to call ec2:TerminateInstances or s3:DeleteBucket
acceptanceCriteria.then: the API call is denied with an AccessDenied error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ECS task execution role successfully pulls an image from ECR at task start
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ECS task definition references the task execution role ARN
acceptanceCriteria.then: the agent pulls the container image from ECR without error using the execution role

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ECS task execution role injects Secrets Manager secrets as environment variables
acceptanceCriteria.given: a task definition secrets block references a Secrets Manager ARN
acceptanceCriteria.then: the container receives the secret value as an environment variable and no plaintext secret appears in the task definition JSON

tasks.summary: Create Jenkins EC2 IAM role with ECR push and ECS deploy permissions
tasks.description: Create an IAM role named loanflow-jenkins-role with an EC2 trust policy. Attach an inline policy granting: ecr:GetAuthorizationToken, ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability, ecr:PutImage, ecr:InitiateLayerUpload, ecr:UploadLayerPart, ecr:CompleteLayerUpload on the six loanflow/* ECR repositories; ecs:UpdateService and ecs:DescribeServices on the dev and production ECS clusters; and secretsmanager:GetSecretValue scoped to /loanflow/jenkins/* secrets only.

tasks.summary: Attach Jenkins IAM role to the Jenkins EC2 instance via instance profile
tasks.description: Create an IAM instance profile wrapping the loanflow-jenkins-role and associate it with the Jenkins EC2 instance. Verify the role is active by running aws sts get-caller-identity on the instance and confirming the returned ARN matches loanflow-jenkins-role.

tasks.summary: Create ECS task execution role with ECR pull and Secrets Manager read permissions
tasks.description: Create an IAM role named loanflow-ecs-execution-role with an ECS tasks trust policy (ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com). Attach the AWS managed policy AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy for ECR pull and CloudWatch log delivery. Add an inline policy granting secretsmanager:GetSecretValue scoped to ARNs matching arn:aws:secretsmanager:*:*:secret:/loanflow/* to allow secrets injection at task startup.

tasks.summary: Document IAM role ARNs for use in ECS task definitions and Jenkinsfile
tasks.description: After both roles are created, record the full IAM role ARNs in a shared configuration reference. The ECS task execution role ARN is referenced in the executionRoleArn field of every ECS task definition. The Jenkins role ARN is used to verify the instance profile attachment. Store both ARNs in Parameter Store at /loanflow/iam/jenkins-role-arn and /loanflow/iam/ecs-execution-role-arn.
